over_the_top.name: Over-the-Top Move
over_the_top.description: Shoulders open before hips, causing outside-to-in swing path
symptoms: Pull shots left of intended flight line
over_the_top.correction: Hips must open the shoulders from P4.1 to P5.0. Focus on lower body leading the downswing.

left_elbow_stall.name: Left Elbow Stall
left_elbow_stall.description: Left elbow stops or stalls at start of downswing instead of adducting toward body median line

left_elbow_distance_from_body.description: Left elbow too far from body

left_elbow_stall.correction: Move entire left arm (not just elbow) up the address baseline. Activate humeral-scapula to move arm independently of body.

incorrect_elbow_position.name: Incorrect Elbow Position at Impact
incorrect_elbow_position.description: Left elbow in Position #2 instead of Position #1 at impact
incorrect_elbow_position.correction: Focus on adduction location, not just position. Practice knock-down shots to feel proper elbow path.

poor_spine_alignment.name: Poor Spine Alignment at Address
poor_spine_alignment.description: Upper spine tilted too far right at setup
symptoms: Pull shots from spine being pulled forward

spine_tilt.description: Spine tilted right instead of slightly left

poor_spine_alignment.correction: Upper torso should have slight tilt LEFT (~5°). Outside of right knee to right shoulder should form straight line.

angle_loss_backswing.name: Loss of Right Arm/Clubshaft Angle
angle_loss_backswing.description: The 110° angle between right arm and clubshaft changes from P2.0 to P4.0
symptoms: Clubshaft wobbles at top of backswing

right_arm_clubshaft_angle.description: Right arm to clubshaft angle changed

angle_loss_backswing.correction: Maintain 110° angle throughout backswing. Focus on left elbow flexion and keeping connection between arms and body.

improper_weight_transfer.name: Improper Weight Transfer
improper_weight_transfer.description: Weight not transferring properly through the three momentum transfers

weight_distribution_left.description: Weight not transferred to left side

improper_weight_transfer.correction: Practice timing all three transfers: clubhead mass, body weight (Hip COG), and arms mass up address baseline.

early_extension.name: Early Extension
early_extension.description: Hips thrust toward ball before impact instead of rotating

hip_slide.description: Too much hip slide toward ball

early_extension.correction: Maintain posture through impact. Feel like chest stays down toward the ball longer. Hips rotate, not thrust.

casting.name: Casting/Early Release
casting.description: Wrist angle released too early in downswing

l_arm_to_clubshaft.description: Wrist angle released too early at P5.0

casting.correction: Maintain wrist hinge longer in the downswing. Feel like you're pulling the grip down toward the ball. Let centrifugal force release the wrists.

insufficient_shoulder_turn.name: Insufficient Shoulder Turn
insufficient_shoulder_turn.description: Shoulders don't rotate enough in backswing

shoulder_rotation.description: Shoulder rotation less than 80° at top

insufficient_shoulder_turn.correction: Focus on rotating shoulders 90° in backswing while maintaining hip resistance. Feel the coil between upper and lower body.

flat_shoulder_plane.name: Flat Shoulder Plane
flat_shoulder_plane.description: Shoulders rotating too flat/horizontally instead of tilted
flat_shoulder_plane.correction: Left shoulder should work down toward the ball in backswing, not just around. Maintain spine angle.

left_elbow_adduction.name: Left Elbow Adduction Drill
left_elbow_adduction.purpose: Improve left elbow position and location in downswing
setup: Set up more bent over from waist and knees
execution: Lower clubhead to ground by lowering hands
Keep left elbow straight
Cock wrists immediately from P1.1 to P2.0
Only go to P3.0 maximum
Adduct left elbow back toward ball
Keep wrists cocked throughout
Transfer weight 4-6 inches to N-0 ball position
left_elbow_adduction.feel: Dramatic difference in left elbow position (#1 instead of #2). Compression on the ball.

three_momentum_transfer.name: Three Momentum Transfer Timing Drill
three_momentum_transfer.purpose: Coordinate the three critical mass/weight transfers
execution: At P3.0, begin moving left elbow up address baseline
Note: Clubhead is still moving toward target
Time hip COG movement with elbow movement
Coordinate all three transfers to peak at impact
three_momentum_transfer.feel: Smooth acceleration through impact. All body parts working together.
three_momentum_transfer.key_insight: If the golf club is moving towards the target from P3.0 to P4.0, you can start to move your left elbow early in the backswing up the address baseline while the clubhead (lagging) is still moving towards the target.

spine_alignment_setup.name: Spine Alignment Setup Drill
spine_alignment_setup.purpose: Establish correct upper body tilt at address
setup: Use mirror or video for feedback
execution: Align outside of right knee to right shoulder in straight line
Feel upper torso tilted slightly LEFT (~5°)
Check Lines #1, #2, #3 relationship (lumbar, thoracic, cervical)
Maintain this 5° tilt through P6.0 and into follow-through
checkpoints: Do NOT set up with upper spine tilted right
Avoid punch shot setup for full shots
Look for reverse C curvature at finish (P9.0)
spine_alignment_setup.result: Higher trajectory on longer club shots

right_arm_angle_maintenance.name: Right Arm/Clubshaft Angle Maintenance Drill
right_arm_angle_maintenance.purpose: Maintain 110° angle from P2.0 to P4.0
execution: Take club to P2.0 (club parallel, toe up)
Check: 110° between right arm and clubshaft
Continue to P4.0 maintaining exact same angle
If clubshaft wobbles at top, angle has changed
right_arm_angle_maintenance.feel: No change in right arm/clubshaft angle. Solid, controlled top of backswing.

humeral_scapula_activation.name: Humeral-Scapula Activation Drill
humeral_scapula_activation.purpose: Learn to move arm independently of body pivot
execution: Extend left arm in front with no elbow flexion
Move arm laterally left, then right
Notice: Shoulder joint (humeral-scapula) performs this
Now at address position, practice moving entire left arm up baseline
Body pivots, but arm moves independently forward
humeral_scapula_activation.key: Do NOT deactivate shoulder joint in downswing. Move entire arm (not just elbow) as fast as possible back in front of body. This is what creates arm SPEED.

knock_down_shot.name: Knock-Down Shot Practice (Seve Style)
execution: Make abbreviated backswing (P3.0 max)
Focus on adducting left elbow toward ball
Keep wrists cocked
Transfer weight through impact
Feel compression on ball
Short follow-through
knock_down_shot.learn: Proper elbow position (#1), Correct adduction location, Ball compression feel, Trajectory control

hip_lead_drill.name: Hip Lead Drill
setup: Can use alignment stick across hips
execution: Make backswing to P4.0
Pause at top
Initiate downswing ONLY with hip rotation
Feel shoulders lag behind hips
Complete swing with proper sequence
hip_lead_drill.feel: Hips clear first, pulling shoulders and arms through. Power from ground up.

lag_retention_drill.name: Lag Retention Drill
lag_retention_drill.purpose: Maintain wrist angle longer in downswing
execution: Make backswing to P4.0
In downswing, feel like hands drop straight down
Maintain 90° wrist angle as long as possible
Let centrifugal force release the club
Do NOT consciously flip wrists
lag_retention_drill.feel: Hands lead clubhead into impact. Compression on the ball.

pump_drill.name: Pump Drill
pump_drill.purpose: Feel proper downswing sequence and lag
execution: Make backswing to P4.0
Start downswing to P5.0
Stop and return to P4.0
Repeat 3 times
On fourth pump, complete the swing
pump_drill.feel: Groove the feeling of proper sequencing. Maintain lag through the pumps.
